The real question is whether `SaveChanges()` is throwing an exception on the in-memory database—maybe there's a primary key conflict if the new student's auto-generated ID is colliding with the seeded student IDs. from 1, which would conflict with the instructor IDs that are already seeded. The value generator in InMemory doesn't track the explicitly set IDs, so when adding new entities without specifying an ID, it starts fresh from 1 and causes collisions.

The fix is straightforward—I need to assign explicit IDs to the test student objects to avoid these conflicts with the existing instructor records. I think the simpler approach is to just use high ID values when creating test objects so they don't collide with the seeded data—EF Core InMemory should accept explicit keys without issue.

So `RedirectToAction` just instantiates a `RedirectToActionResult` with the action name, controller, and route values—nothing fancy there. All **181 tests pass** across the following test suites:

| Test Suite | Tests | Coverage Area |
|---|---|---|
| **ModelTests** | 22 | All domain models, enums, view models |
| **PaginatedListTests** | 13 | Pagination logic, Create factory, navigation flags |
| **NotificationServiceTests** | 22 | Send/receive, FIFO order, message generation, queue mgmt |
| **HomeControllerTests** | 6 | Index, About (grouping), Contact, Error, Unauthorized |
| **StudentsControllerTests** | 30 | Index sort/filter/paging, CRUD with validation |
| **CoursesControllerTests** | 22 | CRUD, image upload validation (ext, size), file cleanup |
| **DepartmentsControllerTests** | 18 | CRUD, concurrency setup |
| **InstructorsControllerTests** | 19 | CRUD, course assignments, dept admin cleanup |
| **NotificationsControllerTests** | 6 | JSON endpoints, queue limits, mark-read |
| **SchoolContextTests** | 15 | Entity queries, relationships, CRUD |
| **DbInitializerTests** | 2 | Seeding + idempotency |

The test project uses **EF Core InMemory** for database isolation and **Moq** for mocking `IWebHostEnvironment`, `ILogger`, `IUrlHelper`, and `IFormFile`.
